next_steps: Perform transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for wall motion abnormalities, valve function, and ejection fraction
Obtain cardiac MRI with gadolinium enhancement to characterize tissue (e.g., fibrosis, edema, inflammation)
Send serologic testing for Lyme disease (Borrelia burgdorferi) and Q fever (Coxiella burnetii)
Perform 24-hour ambulatory cardiac (Holter) monitoring to document the full burden and progression of AV block
Consult infectious disease specialist for guidance on potential zoonotic exposures
Consult cardiology-electrophysiology to evaluate the need for temporary or permanent pacemaker placement
Monitor liver enzymes and consider liver biopsy if infectious and autoimmune panels remain unrevealing
